description: Freshness check — compares a proposed newsletter theme against recently published newsletters to detect repetition, narrative fatigue, and source redundancy.

prompts.instruction:
I have created a text theme for my new newsletter, please, check editorial freshness and if it's not repetitive to last newsletters,

I will provide the text theme text and a link to site where you can find recent newsletters

The text theme:
{theme_body}

Links to my site, on that page, you can find links to recent newsletters, one 3 most recent, and check against them:
https://www.is2digital.com/newsletters

As final output - give your results for this task as a structured output, and tell what to change if needed, try to explain why

<Task_Context>
Evaluate if the proposed newsletter Theme is fresh, non-repetitive, and strategically distinct from the 3 most recent newsletters.
</Task_Context>

<Proposed_Theme>
{theme_body}
</Proposed_Theme>

<Reference_Source>
Navigate to: https://www.is2digital.com/newsletters 
Analyze the content of the three most recent entries.
</Reference_Source>

<Evaluation_Criteria>
1. THEMATIC REPETITION: Is the core narrative essentially the same as a recent issue?
2. FEATURED OVERLAP: Does this theme rely on an article that was already used in the last 3 issues?
3. NARRATIVE FATIGUE: Does the 'angle' (e.g., 'AI Regulation' or 'LLM Efficiency') feel over-rotated? If we just covered this angle 2 weeks ago, flag it as 'Stale'.
4. SOURCE REDUNDANCY: Check if the primary sources/links are the same as the previous week. We aim for diverse sourcing to maintain authority.
</Evaluation_Criteria>

<Output_Format>
Output ONLY a JSON object in this structure:
{
  "isFresh": boolean,
  "confidenceScore": 0.0-1.0,
  "assessment": "Detailed 2-3 sentence explanation of the overlap or uniqueness found.",
  "suggestedChanges": "Specific editorial pivot to make the theme fresher if isFresh is false."
}
</Output_Format>

Perform the check now.
